title: Guardrail: make producer!=approver MECHANICAL in blackboard.py (closes the T-091 slip class)
description: Edit .harness/bin/blackboard.py cmd_update so that '--status done' is REFUSED unless BOTH: (a) the task's current status is 'review' (i.e., it went through a handoff — a claimed/in_progress task can never jump straight to done), and (b) the acting agent (args.agent / ambient) != handoff.from_agent (the producer cannot verdict own work). Escape hatch: --override-producer-check flag, valid ONLY together with a --note explaining why; the refusal and any override must be logged to events.jsonl with an explicit 'agent' field. ALSO (tightly coupled, closes F7 partially): add the acting agent field to the task_updated log_event on ALL status changes so producer!=approver becomes auditable without reconstruction. 'failed' stays allowed from any status (abandonment). Owned file: .harness/bin/blackboard.py ONLY. Acceptance: (1) probe on NEW scratch task T-093 (create it, claim as X, update --status done as X -> REFUSED with clear message; update as unrelated Y while in_progress -> REFUSED because not in review; handoff, update done as X (producer) -> REFUSED; update done as Y -> ACCEPTED); (2) --override-producer-check without --note -> refused; with --note -> works and logs the override; (3) regression: status/show/next/claim/add-task/handoff unchanged; historical board untouched; (4) T-093 disposed (failed) after probing with a closure note. Replayable evidence in handoff.

notes.note: plan: edit cmd_update in blackboard.py to mechanically gate --status done (require prior status=review + actor!=handoff.from_agent, with --override-producer-check+--note escape hatch, logged), add acting agent to task_updated events; probe on new scratch T-093; regression-check read paths + claim/handoff cycle; py_compile before live use

Implemented mechanical producer!=approver guardrail in cmd_update() of .harness/bin/blackboard.py.
--status done now REFUSED unless (a) previous status == 'review' and (b) args.agent != handoff.from.
Escape hatch --override-producer-check (requires --note, both logged to events.jsonl as
producer_check_refused / producer_check_overridden with explicit agent field). task_updated event
now also carries agent=args.agent explicitly (F7). py_compile clean before any live use.

Full probe protocol run against fresh scratch task T-093 (epic scratch-guardrail, disposed as
failed afterward). Exact replay commands and observed results:

1. python3 .harness/bin/blackboard.py claim T-093 --agent probe-x
   -> claimed (ok)
2. python3 .harness/bin/blackboard.py update T-093 --status in_progress --agent probe-x --note "..."
   -> updated (ok)
3. PROBE A python3 .harness/bin/blackboard.py update T-093 --status done --agent probe-x
   -> REFUSED (exit 1): "refused: T-093 cannot go straight to 'done' from 'in_progress'. Mechanical
      guardrail (producer != approver): a task must be handed off for review first --
      `blackboard.py handoff T-093 --to-role verifier --note \"...\"` -- before it can be marked
      done. Pass --override-producer-check together with --note to force it."
4. PROBE B python3 .harness/bin/blackboard.py update T-093 --status done --agent probe-y
   -> REFUSED (exit 1), identical "not in review" message (gate a fires before gate b since status
      isn't 'review' yet).
5. python3 .harness/bin/blackboard.py handoff T-093 --to-role verifier --agent probe-x --note "..."
   -> handed off (ok), status -> review, handoff.from = probe-x.
6. PROBE C python3 .harness/bin/blackboard.py update T-093 --status done --agent probe-x
   -> REFUSED (exit 1): "refused: T-093 was handed off by 'probe-x' -- the producer cannot verdict
      their own work (producer != approver). A different agent must mark it done, or pass
      --override-producer-check together with --note explaining why."
7. PROBE D python3 .harness/bin/blackboard.py update T-093 --status done --agent probe-y --note "..."
   -> ACCEPTED (exit 0): "updated T-093: status=done; note appended to ..."

Reset + override round (update --status open is ungated, works from any status; re-claimed,
in_progress, handed off again by probe-x):
8. python3 .harness/bin/blackboard.py update T-093 --status open --agent probe-reset --note "..."
   -> updated (ok)
9. claim/update in_progress/handoff again as probe-x (identical to steps 1/2/5)
10. PROBE E python3 .harness/bin/blackboard.py update T-093 --status done --agent probe-x --override-producer-check
    -> REFUSED (exit 1): "refused: --override-producer-check requires --note explaining why (the
       reason is recorded to events.jsonl for audit)"
11. PROBE F python3 .harness/bin/blackboard.py update T-093 --status done --agent probe-x --override-producer-check --note "probe: emergency override — testing escape hatch"
    -> ACCEPTED (exit 0), printed "WARNING: producer-check override used on T-093 by probe-x: <refusal reason>"
       then "updated T-093: status=done; ...". events.jsonl has a producer_check_overridden record
       with agent=probe-x, previous_status=review, producer=probe-x, note=<the note>.
12. Disposal: python3 .harness/bin/blackboard.py update T-093 --status failed --agent substrate-worker-1 --note "SCRATCH DISPOSAL: probe complete..."
    -> ACCEPTED (exit 0) from status 'done' — confirms 'failed' remains allowed from ANY status
       (abandonment path unaffected by the guardrail).

events.jsonl verification (grep T-093 | jq): every producer_check_refused / producer_check_overridden
/ task_updated record carries the correct explicit "agent" (probe-x / probe-y / probe-reset /
substrate-worker-1), not the ambient default. Confirmed with:
  grep T-093 .harness/logs/events.jsonl
Sample lines observed (abbreviated):
  {"event":"producer_check_refused","agent":"probe-x","previous_status":"in_progress",...}
  {"event":"producer_check_refused","agent":"probe-y","previous_status":"in_progress",...}
  {"event":"producer_check_refused","agent":"probe-x","previous_status":"review","producer":"probe-x",...}
  {"event":"task_updated","agent":"probe-y","status":"done",...}
  {"event":"producer_check_overridden","agent":"probe-x","previous_status":"review","producer":"probe-x","note":"probe: emergency override — testing escape hatch",...}
  {"event":"task_updated","agent":"probe-x","status":"done","override_producer_check":true,...}

Regression (unchanged read/write paths):
- `python3 .harness/bin/blackboard.py status` -> unchanged format, counts correct (done=28 historical
  + T-093 as an extra failed entry after disposal; no historical task's status mutated).
- `python3 .harness/bin/blackboard.py next --agent regression-check --role worker` -> correctly
  reports "no claimable task for role=worker" (T-031 itself is claimed/in_progress by me, T-093
  disposed) — matches expected DAG state, not a regression.
- `python3 .harness/bin/blackboard.py show T-030` -> historical done task unchanged/untouched.
- Ordinary claim -> in_progress -> handoff -> done-by-different-agent cycle (steps 1/2/5/7 above)
  works exactly as before the change (ACCEPTED once producer!=approver and status==review both hold).
- `python3 -m py_compile .harness/bin/blackboard.py` -> COMPILE_OK, run before any live probe.

Self-referential check for T-031's own future verdict: this handoff sets T-031 to status=review with
handoff.from=substrate-worker-1. The FUTURE verifier (verifier-b, not me) will run
`blackboard.py update T-031 --status done --agent verifier-b`: previous_status will be 'review' and
verifier-b != 'substrate-worker-1', so both guardrail conditions are satisfied and the new mechanism
will ACCEPT that verdict under the exact same code path proven by PROBE D above. If I (substrate-
worker-1) attempted to self-verdict T-031, the same code path as PROBE C would REFUSE it.

Owned file: .harness/bin/blackboard.py only (per task instruction). No other harness files touched.

HANDOFF -> verifier: Mechanical producer!=approver guardrail implemented in .harness/bin/blackboard.py cmd_update(): --status done refused unless previous status==review AND acting --agent != handoff.from; --override-producer-check escape hatch requires --note and is logged (producer_check_refused / producer_check_overridden events with explicit agent field); task_updated now also logs agent=args.agent explicitly (F7); 'failed' remains allowed from any status. Full probe protocol run on fresh scratch T-093 (created + disposed as failed by me) — replay: claim T-093 --agent probe-x; update in_progress; update done --agent probe-x (REFUSED: not in review); update done --agent probe-y (REFUSED: not in review); handoff --to-role verifier --agent probe-x; update done --agent probe-x (REFUSED: producer==approver); update done --agent probe-y (ACCEPTED); update open (reset); re-handoff; update done --agent probe-x --override-producer-check (REFUSED: needs --note); update done --agent probe-x --override-producer-check --note '...' (ACCEPTED + WARNING + producer_check_overridden logged); update failed --agent substrate-worker-1 (ACCEPTED from status=done, proving failed works from any status). Exact refusal/acceptance texts and events.jsonl excerpts are in the detail-file note I just appended. Regression verified: status/next/show unchanged, historical tasks (e.g. T-030) untouched, py_compile clean pre-use. Verifier note: my own attempt to self-verdict T-031 would hit the same refusal path as PROBE C; your verdict (different agent, task now in review) will hit the same accept path as PROBE D.

VERIFIED (verifier-b, independent adversarial replay). Core matrix on scratch T-094: done-from-in_progress refused for both producer(probe-x) and third-party(probe-y); handoff then done-by-producer refused (producer!=approver msg); done-by-different-agent(probe-y) ACCEPTED; override-without-note refused; override-with-note ACCEPTED with WARNING + producer_check_overridden event. ATTACKS: (a) done from open->refused; (b) done from blocked->refused, done from failed->refused; (c) after 2nd agent(Y) re-handoffs a rejected task, original producer X CAN verdict done (handoff.from is now Y) - documented as correct, X is no longer producer-of-record; (d) T-091 slip CLOSED: omitting --agent still refuses straight-to-done from in_progress (status==review gate is agent-independent); ambient identity 'main' recorded EXPLICITLY in producer_check_refused and task_updated events; (e) done on already-done->refused (not in review); (f) failed ACCEPTED from open/in_progress/review/blocked/done - abandonment never gated. F7: every producer_check_refused/overridden/task_updated event carries explicit agent field incl ambient 'main'. Regression: py_compile OK; status/show/next/claim/handoff/add-task shapes unchanged; git diff confined to docstring+cmd_update+parser; HISTORICAL task statuses changed=NONE (done=28 era untouched). Self-referential: this very verdict is the live accept-path proof - verifier-b != substrate-worker-1, task in review, accepted. No holes; guardrail sound.
